evidence_quotes.quote: Taking into consideration games that recruire a "loot box" to be opened in a rogue like to have randomness and chance.
preserve a fully working copy of that game
preserving games into the Digital Fareness act

paragraphs.text: Wanted to give EU a blessing from god since they try to make the world a more reasonable place. Also wanted to talk about the 2 matters in this initiative that stood out to me. Number 1 being the gambling and the limits or restrictions on it. I don't think gambling should be deleted or removed from games since RNG (Random Number Generators) are a good core consept for the games i play. Taking into consideration games that recruire a "loot box" to be opened in a rogue like to have randomness and chance. (makes games more fun and janky the more random events go on). Number 2 being the lack of consideration for games and their lifetime plans. Heres a personal story that still inflicts emotions on my olderself. I was a kid just recently in 2012 - 2018 and I plaid many a game on miniclip and other online videogame store frons and communities. Many game came and went, but there was this one game that i plaid with my whole class called [[redacted so i dont advertise]], and in it we had loads of fun. then a gaming market crash happened, aka the flash games dying. my beloved games, all of them down to everything exept blockgame were deleted and not accesible. I didt buy thoes games and enjoid them for free as a kid. but i know my friends now would have loved to play all the games i playd a long time ago. All i wish is that when i play a game i could buy/download/preserve a fully working copy of that game so that not only me but my friend, my community & my children get to experience that which made my childhood so fun and full of experiences. I am not alone in asking the EU to add the consept of preserving games into the Digital Fareness act so that others might experience joy.
